patches.hypothesis: Refined the patch to handle different types of input arrays, added more test cases to cover edge cases, and avoided hardcoding values in the patch. The solution now uses a more generic approach and includes proper null checks and boundary conditions.
patches.original_hypothesis: The issue is caused by the ArrayStoreException that occurs when trying to store an object of type Object into an array of type T. This is because the `sample` method assumes that the type of the input array is the same as the type of the output array, but in this case, the input array contains objects of type Object and the output array is of type T.
